name: Biscoff Protein Cheesecake
description: Biscoff cookie-butter protein cheesecake, crustless - a flavor variant of the Vanilla Protein Cheesecake base. No cookie-butter emulsion needed: browned butter plus a bloomed speculoos spice blend (toasted cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg) stand in for the emulsion's spiced-cookie note, and 60 g Biscoff (45 g in the batter + a 15 g swirl on top) carries the rest. The base's light cream cheese is swapped to FAT-FREE here because the Biscoff and brown butter now supply the richness - that one swap is what keeps it at ~172 cal/slice (ratio ~10.4) instead of ~205 despite the added cookie butter. Powdered PB deepens the nutty flavor and offsets the Biscoff fat with protein. To-make.

payload.instructionText: Brown the butter: melt the butter in a light-colored pan over medium, swirling, until it foams, then smells nutty and the milk solids turn golden-amber (~2-3 min) - pull the instant it's amber, it goes from nutty to burnt in seconds. Off the heat, stir in the cinnamon plus a pinch each of ground ginger and nutmeg and let them bloom in the hot butter ~30 sec - this is where the speculoos 'cookie' flavor comes from (brown butter alone is nutty, not spiced). Scrape it all, including the toasty brown bits, into a small bowl and cool to barely warm.
Melt 15 g Biscoff 10-15 sec until pourable and drizzle it over the top of the batter. Drag a toothpick through in a few S-curves to marble it - swirl, don't stir it in.

Beat the fat-free cream cheese alone until smooth, then add the yogurt, blended cottage cheese, protein powder, powdered peanut butter, sweetener, vanilla, lemon, the 45 g Biscoff, the cooled browned-butter spice mixture, and the vanilla pudding mix. Sift/whisk in the AP flour smooth (no lumps) and mix just until combined - don't overwork it. Warm the bowl ~15 sec if it clumps.

payload.text: Brown butter goes from nutty-amber to acrid-burnt in seconds. Pull it the moment the milk solids are golden and it smells like toffee; if it smells sharp or the bits are black, start over - burnt butter taints the whole cake.

Bloom the cinnamon, ginger, and nutmeg in the hot butter off the heat - the spices are fat-soluble, so 30 sec in the browned butter wakes them up far more than stirring them into cold batter. Brown butter brings the nutty caramel depth; the bloomed spices are what make it actually read as Biscoff.

This batter is leaner than a classic cheesecake (fat-free cream cheese), so it sets firmer and dries out faster - do NOT overbake. Pull at a just-set center with a slight wobble; chase a fully firm center and it bakes out rubbery.
Swirl the melted Biscoff on top, don't stir it in - a few toothpick S-curves keep a marbled pattern. Blend it all the way in and you just muddy the color and lose the 'Biscoff cheesecake' look.

Crustless by design - the Biscoff and browned butter carry the cookie flavor without a crust's calories. If you want a crust, the Protein Graham Crust sub-recipe slots in like it does on the other cheesecakes (about +50 cal/slice).
